business.headline: Explained: Why India slipped to 6th-largest economy despite strong growthIndia has slipped to the 6th-largest economy in the IMF's latest outlook. Why did it happen despite strong growth? IndiaToday.in spoke to economists to find out.
Sensex ends 500 points higher as HUL leads market rally with 5% riseThe S&P BSE Sensex added 504.86 points to end at 78,493.54, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 156.80 points to close at 24,353.55.

Sebi allows 50% IPO size cut without refiling: What it means for investorsThe regulator has taken this step as market participants are finding it difficult to mobilise resources and access capital markets in the current geopolitical environment.

Akshay Tritiya property plans? Don't miss these 5 key checksAkshay Tritiya is seen as the perfect day to make big financial moves, and property is high on many buyers' lists, but before you take the plunge, are you checking the right things?
DA hike delayed: Union body urges Govt to act quicklyThe timely announcement of DA will help restore confidence and ease concerns among lakhs of central government employees and pensioners.

Netflix shares slide over 9% as co-founder Reed Hastings steps downHastings has been a key figure in shaping Netflix into what it is today. He co-founded the company and played a major role in transforming it from a DVD-by-mail service into a global streaming giant.

Forced to mis-sell: IIIT graduate quits Rs 17 LPA bank job, cites sales pressureChirag Madaan's resignation from his Rs 17 lakh per annum banking job has reignited focus on mis-selling at financial institutions, an issue IndiaToday.in had earlier highlighted through its investigation into how sales targets and incentives shape what customers are sold.

Gautam Adani overtakes Mukesh Ambani to become Asia's richestThe latest ranking places Gautam Adani at the top spot in Asia, with Mukesh Ambani now in second place. Both remain among the top 25 richest people globally, even as their wealth levels have seen changes this year.

2% to 4% DA hike likely: Here's how much your monthly pay may riseA fresh DA revision is around the corner, and early estimates suggest a 2% to 4% hike, but how much extra could this actually add to your monthly salary? Let's have a look.

India slips to 6th-largest economy: What IMF's latest data showsIndia has slipped out of the world's top five economies in nominal GDP terms, going by the IMF's latest World Economic Outlook estimates. The change says more about currency than growth, with projections suggesting the loss in ranking may not last long.

Markets open flat as geopolitical tensions ease, but caution remainsThe BSE Sensex was trading at 78,030.99, up 42.31 points or 0.05% at around 9:43 am. The Nifty 50, however, slipped marginally by 6.85 points or 0.03% to 24,189.90.

Gurugram circle rates increase by up to 75%: How much will home prices rise?The revision in Gurugram circle rates comes at a time when the city continues to see strong demand due to its proximity to the airport and the presence of major offices and industries.

Rs 8 lakh crore stuck: How delayed payments are hurting India's MSMEsAcross India, the total value of such pending payments to micro, small and medium enterprises is estimated at Rs 8.1 lakh crore, according to industry estimates and government assessments.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock markets rise or fall today?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,178 at 8:06 am, suggesting that the Nifty 50 may open close to its previous session's level of 24,196.75. This points to a muted start for the markets after recent volatility.

Gold is rising slowly: Should you expect a big move soon?Gold has been moving up, but quietly. There's no sudden surge, just a gradual rise. So the big question is—does this signal a strong rally ahead or more sideways movement?

IMF warns Asia may face sharper hit from Middle East energy shockAsia relies heavily on imported oil and gas, particularly from the Middle East. This makes it more vulnerable when supply disruptions or price spikes occur.

Gold, equities or debt? Expert says no asset stays on top for longIf you're waiting to invest in the "best" asset, you may be chasing a moving target. An expert explains why gold, equities and debt keep rotating as top performers over time.

NPS, EPF or mutual funds: Where should you invest Rs 5,000 for 10 yearsYou set aside Rs 5,000 every month with one simple goal, i.e., to build something meaningful over 10 years. But the real question is where to invest it. NPS, EPF or mutual funds? Let's have a closer look.

Sensex slips 123 points, Nifty ends below 24,200 as markets erase early gainsThe BSE Sensex closed at 77,988.68, down 122.56 points or 0.16%. The NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,196.75, lower by 34.55 points or 0.14%.

Sensex falls 1,000 points after early gains: What should investors do?The BSE Sensex moved within a wide range of 1,055.39 points, touching a high of 78,730.32 and a low of 77,674.93. The NSE Nifty 50 also saw similar movement, trading in a range of 298.15 points between a high of 24,400.95 and a low of 24,102.80.

entertainment.headline: Prakash Raj jabs Vijay's 'cinema model' politics: Cannot give actors the country Actor Prakash Raj criticised Vijay's political entry, terming it the 'cinema model' of politics. He urged voters to choose between the Dravidian, slave, or cinema models ahead of the 2026 Tamil Nadu elections.

Jailer 2 BTS video leaked online, makers of Rajinikanth's film issue strict warning A leaked BTS clip from Jailer 2, starring Rajinikanth, has surfaced online, prompting a strong response from production house Sun Pictures. Last week, Vijay Jaya Nayagan was released online in HD print.

Hansika Motwani on divorce from Sohael Khaturiya: Better to get off the wrong train Actor Hansika Motwani has opened up about her divorce from Sohael Khaturiya. She shared that she is in a better emotional space and grateful for her family's support during this personal phase.

Dhurandhar revived Bollywood: Kangana praises film, Madhavan's Ajit Doval act Kangana Ranaut doubled down on her praise for Dhurandhar 2. She appreciated R Madhavan's performance in Dhurandhar: The Revenge, while also crediting the film with bringing audiences back to Hindi cinema.

Interview: Woo Do-hwan on Bloodhounds 2, Bond with Lee Min-ho, Kim Min-jae, Mr.Plankton India Remake In an exclusive chat with India Today, actor Woo Do-hwan reflected on the pressure, success, and growing worldwide reach of K-dramas as Bloodhounds Season 2 topped the charts.

RSS role in Gandhi death, Babri events? Sanjay Dutt's Aakhri Sawal raises questions Sanjay Dutt shared new posters of his upcoming film Aakhri Sawal, which question the truth behind India's historical events, including Mahatma Gandhi's assisination and Babri Masjid demolition.

Sydney Sweeney, Scooter Braun make romance Insta-official. His reaction says it all Actor Sydney Sweeney and music producer Scooter Braun went public with their romance on Instagram. After months of speculation, their relationship is now out in the open.

Mr X review: All guns and no glory in Arya-Gautham Karthik's spy thrillerRelease Date:Fri Apr 17, 2026 Mr X movie review: Director Manu Anand's Mr X, starring Arya, Gautham Karthik and Sarathkumar, is a spy thriller that aims to be Tamil cinema's answer to Mission: Impossible, Bourne and Bond films. It ends up being a spoof of all three.

Love and War on Republic Day, Sanjay Leela Bhansali makes new announcement Sanjay Leela Bhansali's Love and War has a new release date, shifting its big-screen arrival to a key holiday window. The film's postponement and revised timing also set up an interesting box office clash.

Aamir Khan shares the story of one day that changed his life forever. Video Aamir Khan shared the story of one day that changed his life. He revealed the incident from the initial days of his acting career.

Bloodhounds star Woo Do-hwan on Indian remake of K-drama and cameo | Exclusive In an exclusive chat with India Today, actor Woo Do-hwan reflected on the pressure, success, and growing worldwide reach of K-dramas as Bloodhounds Season 2 topped the charts. The action-packed sequel defies expectations and has earned praise for surpassing its first season.

Didn't eat a mountain: Patralekhaa blasts paps for body-shaming in viral video Actor Patralekhaa has strongly reacted to a viral video by a paps account that commented on her postpartum body. Calling out the paparazzi for insensitivity, she shared a note urging more understanding of how women's bodies change after childbirth.

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 1 prediction: Akshay's film eyes Jolly LLB 3-like start Bhooth Bangla is eyeing a respectable opening at the box office despite tempered pre-release expectations. With advance bookings and paid previews indicating steady interest, the horror-comedy could land close to the opening numbers of Jolly LLB 3 if momentum holds.

Alia Bhatt shares emotional post as sister Shaheen is engaged to Ishaan Mehra Alia Bhatt's sister Shaheen got engaged to fitness trainer Ishaan Mehra. Alia and other celebrities from the Hindi film industry reacted to the couple's announcement on social media.

Kangana Ranaut reacts to Chirag Paswan dating buzz with a witty remark Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut has set the record straight on her bond with her fellow parliamentarian Chirag Paswan. She clarified that Chirag is her friend and there is no romantic relationship between them.

Bhooth Bangla X reviews: Akshay Kumar-Priyadarshan's comedy gets mixed response Akshay Kumar-Priyadarshan's horror comedy, Bhooth Bangla received a mixed response in its early reviews. The film's paid previews were held on April 16.

Couldn't protect Jana Nayagan, claims to protect Tamil Nadu: DMK MP jabs Vijay The leak of Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan has stirred political controversy with Rajya Sabha MP, P Wilson, questioning the actor's political competence. Meanwhile, the Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested nine suspects and is investigating the breach.

Katy Perry crashes prom in viral video from night of Ruby Rose's claims. Watch The sexual assault allegations made by actor Ruby Rose against singer Katy Perry have sparked widespread attention. A video from the same night is now going viral. Perry has denied the claims by calling them false and harmful.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 29: Only Rs 5 crore away from dethroning Pushpa 2 Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is inching closer to a major global box office milestone, with its latest numbers putting it within striking distance of Pushpa 2: The Rule. Check the latest worldwide collection update inside.

general news.headline: Rahul Gandhi's magician of Balakot swipe at PM draws angry protests from BJP Live updatesCongress MP Rahul Gandhi sparked an uproar in the Lok Sabha after targeting Narendra Modi with a sarcastic jibe, referring to him as the "magician of Balakot, demonetisation and Operation Sindoor," drawing strong objections and protests from BJP members.

Conspiracy to save Nashik godman, parents of TCS case accused Nida Khan claimThe parents of TCS Nashik conversion case accused Nida Khan' have denied all allegations of forcible conversion, calling the case fake and politically motivated. They said she is currently at her in-laws' home in Bhiwandi.

What is the Number 16 riddle that Rahul Gandhi referred to in Parliament?Rahul Gandhi left people guessing after mentioning in his speech the 'Number 16' riddle, while debating on the three bills which will pave the way for women's quota in Parliament. With no explanation offered, social media erupted in speculation over what exactly the Congress leader was hinting at.

People paid Rs 8 crore to run, lift at Hyrox Bengaluru event that has India talkingAround 9,000 people gathered at the Bangalore International Exhibition Centre on the weekend to do workouts one could easily do in a park or gym. Each person paid Hyrox over Rs 9,000 for that. It has sparked a debate over whether Hyrox workouts are merely a status symbol or a shift in how India approaches fitness.

Women's quota bill heads for vote: Does BJP have the numbers?Constitutional amendment bills like the one on women's reservation require a special majority of two-thirds to be passed in the Lok Sabha. The NDA, with 293 MPs, doesn't have the requisite numbers to get its passed on its own.

Leader not present, deputy missing: Raghav Chadha's dig at AAP in Rajya SabhaRaghav Chadha took a sharp dig at his party's Rajya Sabha leadership by highlighting their absence while noting his own presence despite being recently removed as deputy leader.

Wary of India, Pakistan's Nur Khan air base gets surveillance shieldRecent satellite images show new construction at Pakistan's vital Nur Khan air base. Experts said it points to bigger military concerns after India's Operation Sindoor last year, which targeted 11 military sites.

How a wrong turn gave Bombay, not capital Calcutta, India's first passenger trainThe Indian Railways network, which marked its 173rd anniversary on Thursday, started with a 33-km Bombay-Thane line. Calcutta, the British Raj's capital, would have got India's first passenger train, but everything changed after a wrong turn by a ship and a shipwreck.

H-1B visa selection rate hits a high of 75%, thanks to TrumpThe selection rate in the H-1B visa lottery has improved sharply this year, hitting 75% in some instances. The jump from 33% H-1B visa approvals is being reported due to the steep drop in applications, driven by the Donald Trump administration's changes to the visa programme.

Bhooth Bangla review: Akshay Kumar-Priyadarshan's film has few laughs, fewer scaresRelease Date:Fri Apr 17, 2026Bhooth Bangla movie review: The much-anticipated Bhooth Bangla, starring Akshay Kumar and directed by Priyadarshan, falls short of delivering the expected blend of horror and comedy. The film struggles with outdated humour, weak storytelling, and lacks the excitement audiences hoped for.

2% to 4% DA hike likely: Here's how much your monthly pay may riseA fresh DA revision is around the corner, and early estimates suggest a 2% to 4% hike, but how much extra could this actually add to your monthly salary? Let's have a look.

PM and I don't have wife issues: Rahul Gandhi's quip draws laughs in ParliamentWhile speaking in the Lok Sabha, Congress MP Rahul Gandhi was admitting how nearly everyone has had a major female influence in their lives, in the form of relations such as a mother, a sister, or a wife.

IPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals manager escapes ban, fined Rs 1 lakh for using phone at dugoutIPL 2026: BCCI fined RR manager Romi Bhinder Rs 1 lakh and issued a strict warning for using a phone in the dugout during an IPL match, after the ACU found his explanation unsatisfactory.

The BJP game plan is to win big even in defeatThe BJP-led NDA will fall short of the votes required for the passage of the three bills linked to reserving 33% of seats for women. PM Narendra Modi himself spoke about the lack of numbers. So, why is the BJP pushing for the passage of the bills? The plan is a larger victory despite the defeat in Parliament. This is how.

TCS Nashik case accused Nida Khan claims she is pregnant, seeks court reliefEight people have been arrested so far in the BPO harassment and conversion case, while one of the accused, Nida Khan, remained on the run. Her lawyer now says she is in Mumbai and has moved court for anticipatory bail, with the plea likely to be heard in Nashik.

Dhurandhar revived Bollywood: Kangana praises film, Madhavan's Ajit Doval actKangana Ranaut doubled down on her praise for Dhurandhar 2. She appreciated R Madhavan's performance in Dhurandhar: The Revenge, while also crediting the film with bringing audiences back to Hindi cinema.

Twist in conman Sukesh's case, actor Jacqueline Fernandez seeks to turn approverUpon hearing the request, the court informed Jacqueline Fernandez that she would have to make a formal request to the Enforcement Directorate (ED) for the same. The development came months after the Supreme Court refused to cancel the criminal proceedings against Jacqueline in the case.

Explained: Why India slipped to 6th-largest economy despite strong growthIndia has slipped to the 6th-largest economy in the IMF's latest outlook. Why did it happen despite strong growth? IndiaToday.in spoke to economists to find out.

Video: Mamata Banerjee panics as man breaches security to touch her feetWest Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee panicked after a man breached her security during a Jalpaiguri padyatra which raised concerns over crowd control and leader safety.

'Still sleeping?' The most annoying question in Indian homesTell us you've never been judged for sleeping a little extra or taking an additional nap, and we'll stop right here. The truth is, Indians love shaming others for sleep, even though it is one of the most essential things the body needs to function properly.

Sachin Tendulkar breaks down the genius of Shreyas Iyer's gravity-defying catchSachin Tendulkar hailed Shreyas Iyer's stunning relay catch against Mumbai Indians as one of the greatest he has ever seen. The Punjab Kings captain continues his imperious, unbeaten run in IPL 2026.

Who the freak needs more MPs?India doesn't need 307 more MPs to crowd a bigger chamber. What India needs at this moment is the right policies to drive growth, and not more policymakers. It is nothing that 307 additional MPs can achieve that the existing 543 can't.

Live: 16 sounds like Epstein, Congress drops hint on Rahul's Parliament riddleSpecial Parliament Session Live Updates: Chaos erupted in the Lok Sabha as Rahul Gandhi attacked the government over amendments to the Women's Reservation Act, alleging it aims to sideline the caste census and reshape India's electoral map. He also took a swipe at Narendra Modi, calling him the "magician of Balakot, demonetisation and Operation Sindoor," sparking protests from BJP members. Gandhi questioned the intent behind three bills to operationalise 33 per cent reservation for women in Parliament and Assemblies, alongside a proposed delimitation exercise that could expand Lok Sabha seats to 850. Voting is expected around 4 pm. In the Rajya Sabha, Harivansh Narayan Singh was re-elected Deputy Chairman for the third consecutive time. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

sports.headline: IPL 2026: RCB to don green jerseys for clash against DC in Bengaluru RCB are all set to don their green jerseys during their clash against Delhi Capitals on Saturday, April 18. RCB are currently in contention to make it to the playoffs in IPL 2026.

IPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals manager escapes ban, fined Rs 1 lakh for using phone at dugout IPL 2026: BCCI fined RR manager Romi Bhinder Rs 1 lakh and issued a strict warning for using a phone in the dugout during an IPL match, after the ACU found his explanation unsatisfactory.

India Women begin T20 World Cup prep with a five-match series against South Africa SA-W vs IND-W: India Women begin their ICC Women's T20 World Cup preparations with a five-match series against South Africa in Durban. Captain Harmanpreet Kaur stressed the series is crucial for building combinations and momentum, with the team entering the contest in strong form after recent series wins over Australia and Sri Lanka.

MI must return captaincy to Rohit: Ex-India star says Hardik lacking leadership IPL 2026: Former India cricketer Manoj Tiwary has questioned Hardik Pandya's leadership amid MI's poor run this season. He believes handing the captaincy back to Rohit Sharma could be the solution.

Sachin Tendulkar breaks down the genius of Shreyas Iyer's gravity-defying catch Sachin Tendulkar hailed Shreyas Iyer's stunning relay catch against Mumbai Indians as one of the greatest he has ever seen. The Punjab Kings captain continues his imperious, unbeaten run in IPL 2026.

IPL 2026: Canteen worker arrested for selling 100 RCB tickets in black market IPL 2026: A Chinnaswamy stadium canteen worker was arrested in Bengaluru after 100 IPL tickets were seized in a black marketing crackdown. The incident adds to growing concerns over transparency in ticket distribution in Karnataka.

T20 World Cup probe: ICC investigates Cricket Canada over alleged corruption The ICC has launched an anti-corruption probe into Cricket Canada, including scrutiny of a T20 World Cup match against New Zealand, after a documentary raised serious allegations.

Iyer's absence from T20 side and leadership group is India's loss: R Ashwin IPL 2026: Ravichandran Ashwin has criticised selectors for leaving Shreyas Iyer out of India's T20 setup, calling it a loss for the team, not the player. Iyer has responded with outstanding form for Punjab Kings, continuing his strong performances across formats.

Varun Chakravarthy to emerge stronger: Kartik Tyagi ahead of KKR against GT IPL 2026: Kartik Tyagi has backed Varun Chakravarthy to overcome his poor start to the IPL season, saying tough phases help players grow. With KKR struggling early, the team will hope Chakravarthy regains form against Gujarat Titans to revive their campaign.

Messi v Ronaldo as club owners? Argentine star acquires Catalan club UE Cornella Lionel Messi has acquired 100 per cent of UE Cornella, a Barcelona-based club known for producing stars like Andre Onana and Jordi Alba, mirroring Cristiano Ronaldo's recent stake in UD Almeria.

Wealth of knowledge: How Hayden is guiding GT's Glenn Phillips in IPL 2026 Glenn Phillips is picking the brain of legendary coach Matthew Hayden to master the mental game and find his process zone ahead of the Gujarat Titans' high-stakes IPL 2026 clash against KKR.

How Arshdeep snapped back to form vs MI: Ponting reveals chat behind the scenesIPL 2026: Arshdeep Singh's return to form came down to clarity, not just skill. A timely chat with Ricky Ponting helped him reset and deliver when PBKS needed him most.

More misery for KKR? Rahane's side set to face spin test vs Gujarat TitansIPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders travel to Ahmedabad to play Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans. It will not be easy for Ajinkya Rahane's side who face in-form Rashid Khan and Prasidh Krishna.

Fatigue post-T20 World Cup? Ashwin flags concern for Mumbai Indians' IPL seasonR Ashwin warns that T20 World Cup fatigue is hitting Mumbai Indians' stars after a 4th IPL 2026 loss. Hardik Pandya hints at "tough calls" as MI struggles to find answers with the ball.

Kolkata disaster: Is Rahane only one to be blamed for KKR's IPL 2026 flopshowKKR are in turmoil and the blame currently seems to be firmly on Ajinkya Rahane and his methods. But is the KKR skipper just the only reason that they find themselves at the bottom of the IPL points table?

No pressure on Bumrah: Coach Jayawardene says MI bowling lacking collectivelyIPL 2026: MI head coach Mahela Jayawardene has backed Jasprit Bumrah despite his wicketless run this season. However, he admitted Mumbai Indians' bowling struggles are a collective issue, not an individual one.

Grinning ear to ear: Preity Zinta relishing PBKS's dominant win over MIIPL 2026: Preity Zinta couldn't hide her joy after PBKS' dominant win over MI, saying she was "grinning ear to ear." The co-owner celebrated the performance with a heartfelt post as Punjab Kings continued their unbeaten run.

Kohli likes another Instagram influencer's photo, fans flood comments sectionIPL 2026: A simple Instagram like by Virat Kohli sparked a viral "algorithm" meme fest online. Fans quickly revived his old explanation, turning yet another social media moment into light-hearted humour.

All-round Arshdeep Singh: Viral reels spiking Punjab Kings' fanbase, says pacerArshdeep Singh took some credit for the spike in Punjab Kings' fan base, saying that his social media game is one of the reasons behind the increase in followers for the franchise. Arshdeep picked up three wickets in the win against MI.

IPL Play of the Day: Ice-cold Shreyas Iyer proves he is the new King of ChasesMI vs PBKS: Shreyas Iyer downed Mumbai Indians with a terrific innings of 66 off 35 balls. This was the third straight match where Shreyas hit a fifty in the chase, helping the team remain unbeaten this season.

Trouble in MI? Jasprit Bumrah's reaction to Hardik Pandya's captaincy call goes viralThings don't seem to be good inside the Mumbai Indians camp as a viral video shows Jasprit Bumrah being unhappy at the call made by Hardik Pandya during the loss to Punjab Kings at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday, April 16.

Vinod Kambli is fine: Wife addresses health speculationFormer cricketer Vinod Kambli's wife, Andrea Hewitt, has dismissed speculation around his health, stating that the former India batter is 'fine'. Her statement comes amid reports suggesting Kambli was battling serious medical issues and remained at risk of a brain stroke.

Frustrated Hardik Pandya slams teammates after embarrassing loss vs Punjab KingsMI vs PBKS: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya cut a frustrated figure after the team's loss against Punjab Kings at the Wankhede Stadium. Asked what went wrong in the game, Hardik said they were outplayed by the visiting team on Thursday, April 16.

Prabhsimran-powered Punjab stay unbeaten, hand Mumbai a reality checkPrabhsimran's 80 and a captain's knock from chase master Shreyas Iyer was enough for Punjab Kings to remain unbeaten in the IPL 2026 campaign and pile on the misery on Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium.

Rs 1cr bargain: Quinton de Kock hits 53-ball ton in timely reminder to MI coachesIPL 2026, MI vs PBKS: Getting his first chance of the season after an injury to Rohit Sharma, Quinton de Kock proved he is not suited for warming the benches. The veteran wicketkeeper-batter smashed 112 off 60 balls, making an immediate impact for the Mumbai Indians.

Manchester City captain Bernardo Silva to leave the club at end of seasonManchester City captain Bernardo Silva will depart the club at the end of the season after the expiry of his contract. Bernardo has won 19 major honours with City, including a Champions League title.

Shreyas Iyer defies gravity for relay catch in Mumbai, Rohit's reaction goes viralShreyas Iyer stunned the Mumbai crowd on Thursday, April 16, pulling off a ridiculous save at the boundary line. Shreyas' gravity defying effort resulted in Hardik Pandya's dismissal in Mumbai Indians vs Punjab Kings.

MS Dhoni sparks frenzy in Hyderabad, fans throng airport for a glimpse. VideoMS Dhoni received a rousing welcome in Hyderabad as he travelled for his first away game of IPL 2026. The Chennai Super Kings star's wife shared videos of fan frenzy at the Hyderabad airport on Instagram on Thursday.

Jay Shah named in prestigious Young Global Leaders by World Economic ForumJay Shah has been named in the Young Global Leaders list by the World Economic Forum. The forum recognised Shah for his growing influence in global cricket and his role in expanding the sport through the International Cricket Council.

MI vs PBKS: Suryakumar fails again as India captaincy rumours continue to swirlMI vs PBKS, IPL 2026: Suryakumar Yadav was dismissed for a golden duck by Arshdeep Singh in Mumbai on Thursday. The T20 World Cup-winning captain has struggled for consistency in the new season of the IPL.

Huge blow for MI: Injured Rohit sidelined for more than one game in IPL 2026Hardik Pandya has revealed that Rohit Sharma will be out for at least a couple of games as he failed to recover in time for the clash against PBKS.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
